abstract.text: a single ticket (typically a single seat, unless general admission)
legal Terms and Conditions (a long string),  ex: “This ticket is a revocable license…”
a single ticket (typically a single seat, unless general admission)
current status of the ticket (across Transfer and Resale systems), ex: “TRANSFER PENDING”

per ticket fees, shown on back of ticket

online streaming ticket (not to a physical Event)
Ticket Type enum (human readable), ex: “Standard Ticket”

which venue entry gate is best to find user’s seats, ex: “VIRTUA LAWN”

Implementation of Ticketmaster post-purchase functionality such as viewing purchased Events, Tickets, and Barcodes.
is barcode is available for rendering?

encoded order identifier used to interface with other services (like webpages)

Confirmed, Completed, TicketNotification, Failed, Refunded
US: Ticket Physical Delivery Service being used (if any), ex: “USPS”, “NONE”
UK: Delivery Type being used for Delivery.type, ex: “mail”,“pdf”,“ups”, “MOBILE”
Ticket represents a range of seats, FROM (ie. including), ex: “17”
More actions status, AVAILABLE or NOT AVAILABLE
purchase date, formatted: yyyy-MM-dd’T’HH:mm:ss.SSSZ, ex: “2022-01-17T16:47:29.000Z”
order id for this ticket (an event can have multiple orders, this ticket is from this order), ex: “3-25412/PHI”
Ticket source system (UK only), ex: “HOST”
